title: Maa Tara Chandi Temple
tagline: Maa Tara Chandi Temple — spiritual town of Bihar

overview.short: Maa Tara Chandi Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Maa Shakti or Maa Durga, located in Sasaram, Bihar, India.
overview.description: Maa Tara Chandi Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Maa Shakti or Maa Durga, located in Sasaram, Bihar, India. It is one of the 51 Shakta pithas.

topPlaces.name: Sasaram
topPlaces.description: Sasaram (), also spelled as Shahasaram and Sasseram, is a historical city and a municipal corporation region in the Rohtas district of Bihar state in eastern India. The city served as the capital of the Sur dynasty during Sher Shah Suri's rule over India in the 16th century, and was residence place sub capital of epic monarch Sahasrabahu (Kartivirya Arjuna).

topPlaces.name: Mokar
topPlaces.description: Mokar is a Village located in Sasaram tehsil of Rohtas district in Bihar, India. It is situated 5 km away from Sasaram, which is both district & sub-district headquarter of Mokar village.

topPlaces.name: Tomb of Hasan Khan Suri
topPlaces.description: The Tomb of Hasan Khan Suri, also known as Sukha Rauza, is a tomb in Sasaram, Bihar, India. It is listed as a monument of national importance.

topPlaces.name: Tomb of Sher Shah Suri
topPlaces.description: The Tomb of Sher Shah Suri is a mausoleum located in Sasaram, in the Indian state of Bihar. Sher Shah Suri, who was the founder of the Sur Empire, commissioned the tomb for himself.

items.note: Sasaram (), also spelled as Shahasaram and Sasseram, is a historical city and a municipal corporation region in the Rohtas district of Bihar state in eastern India. The city served as the capital of the Sur dynasty during Sher Shah Suri's rule over India in the 16th century, and was residence place sub capital of epic monarch Sahasrabahu (Kartivirya Arjuna).
Mokar is a Village located in Sasaram tehsil of Rohtas district in Bihar, India. It is situated 5 km away from Sasaram, which is both district & sub-district headquarter of Mokar village.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Maa Tara Chandi Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Maa Tara Chandi Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Maa Tara Chandi Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Gaya International Airport (96 km) and the nearest railway station is Gaya Junction (99 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Maa Tara Chandi Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Maa Tara Chandi Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Maa Tara Chandi Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Maa Tara Chandi Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Maa Tara Chandi Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sasaram, Mokar, Tomb of Hasan Khan Suri, Tomb of Sher Shah Suri.
